subject: Physics
title: Bose-Einstein Condensate
description: Learn Bose-Einstein Condensate in one minute.

hook.text: Cool atoms to near absolute zero and they merge into a single quantum entity. A fifth state of matter.

buildup.text: Einstein and Bose predicted this in 1924. At extreme cold, atoms lose individuality and behave as one.

discovery.text: In 1995, scientists created it by cooling rubidium atoms to 170 nanokelvins—billionths of a degree above zero.

twist.text: The condensate behaves like a superfluid—flowing without friction and climbing up container walls.

climax.text: Eric Cornell and Carl Wieman won the 2001 Nobel Prize for creating it. Quantum physics made visible.

punchline.text: Atoms merge into one. A new state of matter at the coldest temperatures.

quiz.question: What happens to atoms in a Bose-Einstein condensate?
options: They merge into a single quantum entity and behave as one
They break apart into subatomic particles
They vibrate at extremely high frequencies
